reasons.text: Chen Group owes you $8,000 and is 12 days late.
5 underused software subscriptions are being paid for.

modalContent.title: HOW WOULD THIS IMPACT CASH FLOW?
body: Canceling or pausing Clickup, Notion, Mailchimp, iStock would free up an estimated $180/month from the very next bill cycle.
You can keep that cash in the bank to raise your projected lowest balance, or redirect it toward growth without increasing total burn.

reasons.text: You're spending more on payroll than your revenue is growing.

modalContent.title: HOW WOULD THIS IMPACT CASH FLOW?
body: Your projected lowest balance is $42,500, only $2,500 above your $40,000 target buffer.
Slowing hiring and trimming hours could save $500–1,200/month and keep your burn rate proportional to income.

suggestedNextSteps.text: Pick one main tool that's most beneficial, pause the others (Clickup, Notion, Mailchimp), and redirect that money into something that moves the business forward.
Slow down hiring, trim hours where possible, and make sure future payroll increases are tied to revenue growth.
Collect on overdue invoices before $345.25 in upcoming bills hit. Send reminders to The Chen Group, Electronic Arts.

cashRedirects.text: Add the monthly savings directly into an emergency-like reserve.
Redirect the money into the best-performing ad or content channels (e.g., paid search, targeted social ads, content creation) to accelerate sign-ups or sales.
Use the savings to buy or build one productive tool (e.g., an automation that reduces manual work) that compounds savings over time, even if it costs something upfront.

cr1.description: Keeping $100/month in reserve raises your projected lowest cash balance over the next 14 days by about $70, moving you further toward your target buffer.

cr2.description: Redirecting $100/month to ads could generate new revenue within 30–60 days, gradually improving your cash position if conversion rates hold.

cr3.description: Investing $100/month in a productivity tool may reduce manual hours and lower indirect costs over 60–90 days, providing a compounding effect on your cash buffer.
